channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Latency stalls at 500ms: RAG pipelines break here first
item.description: Cut RAG latency 81%: p95 from 500 ms to 95 ms with async embedding isolation, Redis backpressure queue, and HNSW tuning. Real AWS costs, code, and metrics for 2026.

item.title: Stop sharing a database for multi-tenant SaaS
item.description: Save 40% on SaaS database costs by isolating top 10 tenants first — real RDS bills, PostgreSQL 15, and Aurora Serverless v2 numbers from Jakarta and Manila systems in 2026.

item.title: When simplicity beats the fancy stack
item.description: Cut API latency 88% and AWS bill 82% by rolling back microservices, GraphQL, and Kafka — the real cost of over-engineering with 2026 data and code.

item.title: Misjudged DB pool size? Check these
item.description: Cut database costs 40% by tuning pool size — PostgreSQL 16 and MySQL 8.0 benchmarks show why max pool size 2x too high burns CPU and latency

item.title: Portfolio that hires you: ship first, polish last
item.description: Ship a production-like project in 3 months and land remote jobs from Africa — real examples, cost breakdowns, and incident post-mortems that hiring teams actually read.

item.title: 2026 AI salary leap: prompt vs production
item.description: Cut AI salary lag in 2026: prompt-only vs production stacks — real benchmarks, cost math, and the exact skill that raises pay 28–42 %.

item.title: Why your RAG pipeline fails in prod
item.description: Drop 95th percentile RAG latency from 4.2 s to 320 ms and cut AWS costs 85% by moving embeddings on-device and using Qdrant 1.8.0 with semantic filters.

item.title: Get paid fairly: salary negotiation for Latin devs
item.description: Turn 'global budget' into a fair remote salary in 30 minutes — example numbers, scripts, and negotiation templates for Latin developers.

item.title: Pick WebSocket vs SSE vs long-polling
item.description: Compare WebSockets vs SSE vs long polling with 2026 latency benchmarks and AWS cost sheets—choose the right real-time protocol for your stack

item.title: AI coders ship real products now
item.description: Cut production latency 60% with Neon Postgres and Bun — the 6 tools that turned AI side-projects into real products in 2026

item.title: Battle-tested solo dev tools that survive prod
item.description: Cut API latency 46% and AWS bill 54% with pg-pool, dns-cache, and pino—version-pinned tools for solo devs shipping production code in 2026.

item.title: Pool size setting that kills performance
item.description: Cut database latency 5× by ditching the threads×cores pool myth — with Node 20, PostgreSQL 16, and Prisma 5.3.1 benchmarks and fixes.

item.title: Ship proof, not code
item.description: Cut lead time to remote hire 50% by focusing on proof, not polish — build a working system with CI logs and incident reports

item.title: Non-coders shipped products in 2026
item.description: Ship production-ready apps in weeks with these 3 AI tools — real benchmarks, cost breakdowns, and the mistakes teams keep making in 2026.

item.title: Pick realtime tech in 2 minutes
item.description: Compare WebSockets vs Server-Sent Events vs long polling with 2026 latency, CPU, and cost numbers — pick the right tool in minutes.

item.title: Production RAG pipelines’ invisible bottlenecks
item.description: We expected 500 daily queries; the first traffic spike hit 12,000 in two hours after a marketing blitz.

item.title: 2026 AI salaries: prompt vs vector vs RAG
item.description: In 2026, prompt tuning pays $152k while RAG pipelines pay $210k — but only if latency stays <300 ms. Compare latency, cost, and tooling for prompt vs vector vs RAG in this data-backed breakdown.

item.title: Over-engineering costs $12k/year — here’s the fix
item.description: Cut monthly AWS bill 98% with a single-process architecture — latency dropped 86%, support tickets vanished. Real numbers from a 2025 production rebuild.

item.title: Pay raise script for remote devs in 2026
item.description: 24% average uplift for remote devs in 2026 — how to counter offers using Levels.fyi, a Python calculator, and a three-paragraph email template that closes in 11 days on average.

item.title: Cache Showdown: When Redis Fails and Memcached Wins
item.description: Cut API response time 70% with Memcached caching — connection pooling, eviction policies, and the failover mistake most teams make in 2026.
